title: Cleanup: Delete 6 stale Graphiti edges via FalkorDB temporal-range query
analysis: Task requires deleting 6 stale Graphiti edges from FalkorDB that were created by reconciliation runs a3715f42/73e7410b between 2026-03-22 17:50-18:15 UTC. Semantic search fails because these edges embed poorly. The existing GraphitiBackend already has a well-established pattern for direct Cypher queries (using `cast(Any, client.driver)._get_graph(None)` + `await graph.query(cypher, params)`), as seen in `query_stale_node_embeddings`, `query_stale_edge_embeddings`, `get_node_text`, `get_edge_text`, etc. The approach is: (1) Add two new methods to GraphitiBackend — `query_edges_by_time_range` for temporal-range queries and `bulk_remove_edges` for batch deletion by UUID list. (2) Create a maintenance script `cleanup_stale_edges.py` following the pattern of `reindex.py` (standalone CLI-callable async script with CleanupManager class). (3) Write tests following the exact mock pattern from `test_reindex.py` (`_make_backend`, `_make_graph_mock`, patching `cast`). The edge relationship type in FalkorDB is `RELATES_TO` (confirmed from all existing Cypher queries in graphiti_client.py). The conftest.py `mock_config` fixture provides a FusedMemoryConfig without real API keys.

steps.description: Write failing tests for GraphitiBackend.query_edges_by_time_range(start, end). Add TestQueryEdgesByTimeRange class in test_cleanup_stale_edges.py with tests: (1) test_returns_matching_edges — mock graph returns rows with [uuid, fact, name, valid_at, invalid_at], verify returned list of dicts; (2) test_returns_empty_when_no_matches — mock returns empty result_set; (3) test_raises_when_not_initialized — client is None, expect RuntimeError; (4) test_passes_time_params_to_query — verify start/end passed as Cypher params. Use _make_backend/_make_graph_mock pattern from test_reindex.py.

Implement GraphitiBackend.query_edges_by_time_range(start: str, end: str) -> list[dict]. Uses the existing _get_graph pattern: MATCH ()-[e:RELATES_TO]->() WHERE e.valid_at >= $start AND e.valid_at <= $end RETURN e.uuid, e.fact, e.name, e.valid_at, e.invalid_at. Returns list of dicts with keys uuid, fact, name, valid_at, invalid_at for human review.

Write failing tests for GraphitiBackend.bulk_remove_edges(uuids). Add TestBulkRemoveEdges class with tests: (1) test_deletes_matching_edges — mock graph.query, verify correct Cypher with UUID list, returns count; (2) test_handles_empty_uuid_list — empty list returns 0 without calling query; (3) test_raises_when_not_initialized — client is None, expect RuntimeError; (4) test_passes_uuid_list_to_query — verify UUIDs passed as $uuids param.

Implement GraphitiBackend.bulk_remove_edges(uuids: list[str]) -> int. Uses Cypher: MATCH ()-[e:RELATES_TO]->() WHERE e.uuid IN $uuids DELETE e. Returns 0 for empty list without querying. Logs UUIDs being deleted at INFO level.

Write failing tests for CleanupManager class and CleanupResult dataclass. Add TestCleanupManager class with tests: (1) test_find_stale_edges_delegates_to_backend — calls backend.query_edges_by_time_range with start/end; (2) test_cleanup_queries_and_deletes — calls find then bulk_remove, returns CleanupResult with edges_found and edges_deleted; (3) test_dry_run_queries_but_does_not_delete — dry_run=True calls find but skips bulk_remove, edges_deleted=0; (4) test_cleanup_empty_result — no edges found, no deletion attempted.

Implement CleanupManager and CleanupResult in maintenance/cleanup_stale_edges.py. CleanupResult dataclass with edges_found, edges_deleted, edge_details fields. CleanupManager takes a GraphitiBackend, has find_stale_edges(start, end) and cleanup(start, end, dry_run=False) methods. Following reindex.py pattern.

Write failing tests for run_cleanup async entrypoint. Add TestRunCleanup class with tests: (1) test_loads_config_and_runs_cleanup — mocks FusedMemoryConfig/MemoryService/CleanupManager, verifies cleanup called with correct start/end/dry_run; (2) test_closes_service_on_success — verify service.close() called; (3) test_closes_service_on_error — verify service.close() called even when cleanup raises; (4) test_passes_dry_run_flag — verify dry_run=True propagated.

Implement run_cleanup(start, end, config_path=None, dry_run=False) async function and __main__ CLI block. Follows reindex.py pattern: load config, create MemoryService, initialize, create CleanupManager, run cleanup, close in finally. argparse with --start, --end, --dry-run, --config flags. Default start/end to the known stale window (2026-03-22T17:50:00 / 2026-03-22T18:15:00).

design_decisions.decision: Add query_edges_by_time_range and bulk_remove_edges to GraphitiBackend rather than putting raw Cypher in the maintenance script
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing pattern where all FalkorDB access is encapsulated in GraphitiBackend (see query_stale_node_embeddings, get_edge_text, etc). Keeps Cypher queries testable via the established mock pattern and reusable for future maintenance tasks.

design_decisions.decision: Use string-based time range parameters (ISO 8601 strings) rather than datetime objects
design_decisions.rationale: FalkorDB/Graphiti stores valid_at as string properties on edges. String comparison in Cypher is simpler and avoids serialization edge cases. The task description already specifies the time window as ISO-like strings.

design_decisions.decision: Include a dry_run mode in the cleanup script
design_decisions.rationale: The task requires human review of found edges before deletion. Dry run allows querying without deleting, then a second run without --dry-run performs the actual deletion. This is safer for an IRREVERSIBLE operation.

design_decisions.decision: Create a dedicated CleanupManager class rather than inline functions
design_decisions.rationale: Mirrors the ReindexManager pattern from reindex.py. Separates orchestration logic from backend access, making each independently testable.

design_decisions.decision: Use RELATES_TO as the edge type in Cypher queries
design_decisions.rationale: All existing edge queries in graphiti_client.py use RELATES_TO — this is the Graphiti-standard relationship type for entity edges (facts).

design_decisions.decision: Default start/end to the known stale window in CLI args
design_decisions.rationale: This task targets a specific incident (2026-03-22 17:50-18:15 UTC). Defaulting CLI args to this window makes the immediate fix a zero-argument invocation while keeping the script reusable for future cleanups.

design_decisions.decision: bulk_remove_edges returns early for empty UUID list without querying FalkorDB
design_decisions.rationale: Defensive programming — avoids a no-op DELETE query and makes the behavior explicit. Empty list = 0 deleted, guaranteed.

reuse.what: GraphitiBackend._get_graph pattern for direct Cypher queries
reuse.how: Use `cast(Any, client.driver)._get_graph(None)` then `await graph.query(cypher, params)` — identical to query_stale_node_embeddings, get_edge_text, etc.
Replicate _make_backend (injects mock client), _make_graph_mock (creates result_set mock), and `patch('fused_memory.backends.graphiti_client.cast')` for all backend method tests.
Follow same structure: async entrypoint loads config, creates MemoryService, initializes, runs operation, closes in finally block. argparse for CLI args.
Use the existing mock_config fixture for all tests — provides FusedMemoryConfig without real API keys.
Call self._require_client() at the start of new methods to ensure initialization, matching the pattern of all existing public methods.
CleanupManager / CleanupResult mirror this structure: dataclass for results, class wrapping backend operations, async methods for find and cleanup.
